content: Turmeric added to almond milk reduces inflammation by targeting nuclear factor Kappa B.
Carrageenan, found in some almond milks, is highly inflammatory and should be avoided in a ketogenic diet.
A tablespoon and a half of turmeric is added to the almond milk mixture for anti-inflammatory effects.
Cinnamon mimics insulin action and helps regulate blood sugar, promoting an anti-inflammatory lifestyle.
Ginger powder is included for its digestive and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in reducing chronic joint pain.
Ghee is used instead of regular butter due to its lack of milk solids and higher nutrient density, providing richness to the pudding.
Four tablespoons of chia seeds are added to the almond milk mixture, creating a pudding-like consistency with minimal net carbs.
The final product is a low-carb, anti-inflammatory chia pudding with potential benefits for brain health and satiety.
